steps.title: Keep Rostock essential
steps.content: The sentence needs the subject "Hanseatic League member city Rostock" before the verb "was." The proper noun identifies which member city is being discussed, so it is essential information, not a removable aside.

steps.title: Avoid commas in the subject
steps.content: A puts a comma between the subject and verb. B marks "Rostock" as nonessential and also creates a subject split. C starts the same nonessential structure but does not close it before the verb. No punctuation is needed between "city" and "Rostock." The answer is D.
